sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Get your braking done just before the 100m board and shift down to 2nd gear. Stay tight to the left barrier and clip the inside kerb without mounting it too aggressively. Be gentle on the throttle — traction is limited here, especially with a heavy car in race trim. On exit, let the car drift wide, but not beyond track limits — the wall comes up fast.

This is a flat-out blast uphill. Position the car to the left as you prepare for Massenet, a sweeping, blind left-hander taken in 5th gear. Keep smooth steering input; if you are too aggressive here, you will clip the inside wall or understeer into the barrier on the right.

Lift slightly for the right-hand flick, keeping in 4th gear. Hug the inside barrier as close as you dare and quickly shift focus to the tight left that follows. No room for error — this is a rhythm-based section. Stay composed, and avoid hopping the kerbs or making jerky inputs.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake just past the crest, down to 2nd gear, and rotate the car tightly. Get as close to the inside barrier as possible without contact — this helps square off the turn for a cleaner exit.

This is the slowest corner on the F1 calendar. Full steering lock, down to 1st gear. Brake early, and stay patient. Late apex here helps unwind the wheel sooner. No need for speed — remain tight to the inside and scrub as much speed off your mid-corner speed as possible.

Climb slightly uphill and brake into Mirabeau Bas, a downhill right. Stay in 2nd gear, feather the brake, and hug the right curb tightly. Next comes Portier — a medium-speed right corner taken in 3rd gear. Focus on a clean exit here — this sets you up for the tunnel. Apply throttle early but carefully.

One of the few flat-out zones. Keep it pinned in 7th gear, stay left, and set up for the next heavy braking zone. Prepare to brake in a straight line as you exit the tunnel.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Brake hard just past the 100m board, from 7th down to 2nd gear. The car will be unsettled due to tunnel compression, so be gentle with downshifts and brake inputs. Cut the first part of the chicane modestly, but avoid taking too many penalties, and oversteering is common. Straighten up quickly for the second part and accelerate smoothly.

Fast and blind. Stay left and commit to a clean entry in 5th gear. Avoid both the inside and outside walls by placing the car precisely on the apex. You can lift slightly but keep your momentum up, or at least as much as you can without running wide into the wall.

A rapid right-left-right-left combo follows; enter flat out or with a slight lift, jumping the first apex aggressively, then quickly switch left and jump the second. Downshift to 4th gear, keep it balanced and avoid unsettling the rear. For the second half of the section, dab the brakes, cut the inside curbs, and accelerate out carefully — track limits are tight here.

Brake hard into this tight right, down to 1st gear. Hug the inside curb. Avoid rolling into the barrier mid-corner — its tighter than it looks. Short-shift if needed to avoid wheelspin on exit.

Final corner. Stay far right, then brake and turn in sharply in 2nd gear. Clipping the inside wall is common — leave a bit of margin. Throttle early and hard to open the lap. DRS opens as you cross the line.
